title: Garnishing Preparations for Relevés and Entrées

entries.title: POTATO CROQUETTES
ingredients: 2 lb (907 g) of potatoes, peeled and quartered
instructions: Cook the potatoes quickly in salted water. As soon as they are soft, drain them, and dry them in the front of the oven for a few minutes.
Press the potatoes through a sieve onto a cloth.
Place the purée in a sautépan; season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg; add the butter, and stir over a brisk fire until the purée becomes a consistent paste.
Take off the fire, complete with the egg yolks, mixing well.
Turn the paste out onto a buttered dish in a rather thin layer to cool quickly. Butter the surface to prevent drying.
Take equal portions of this paste (about 1.5 oz or 43 g each) and roll them on a flour-dusted board into the shape of a cork, a ball, or a quoit.
Dip these into an Anglaise and roll in bread-crumbs or raspings, patting the coating on well. (Recipe 174)
Plunge into hot fat and fry until they have acquired a fine, golden colour.

entries.title: DAUPHINE POTATOES
ingredients: 1 lb (454 g) of potato croquette paste (Recipe 219)
6 oz (170 g) of pâte à choux without sugar (Recipe 2374)
instructions: Prepare the potato paste as for croquettes. (Recipe 219)
Add the pâte à choux without sugar and mix the two constituents thoroughly. (Recipe 2374)
Mould in the shape of small cylinders, and treat them à l’Anglaise, like the croquettes. (Recipe 219)

entries.title: DUCHESSE POTATOES
entries.introduction: These are the same as the croquettes, though they are differently treated. (Recipe 219)
instructions: Prepare the potato paste as for croquettes. (Recipe 219)
Shape the paste on a floured board into diminutive cottage-loaves, little shuttle-shaped loaves, small quoits, lozenges, or rectangles.
Gild with beaten egg.
If shaped as quoits, rectangles, or lozenges, streak them with a small knife to prevent the gilding from blistering.
Bake in the oven for a few minutes before using them to dress the dishes they accompany.

entries.title: MARQUISE POTATOES
ingredients: 6 oz (170 g) of very red, reduced tomato-purée
instructions: Take 1 lb (454 g) of croquette paste and add the tomato purée. (Recipe 219)
Pour this mixture into a bag fitted with a large, grooved pipe.
Squeeze it out upon a baking-tray in shapes resembling large meringues.
Slightly gild their surfaces with beaten egg, and put them into the oven for a few minutes before using them to dress the dish.

entries.title: ORDINARY OR DRY DUXELLE
entries.introduction: The uses of Duxelle are legion.
ingredients: 1 tbsp (15 ml) of butter and oil mixed
4 tbsp (60 ml) of mushroom stalks and parings, chopped and pressed
instructions: Slightly fry the onions in the butter and oil mixture.
Add the chopped and pressed mushroom stalks and parings.
Stir over a brisk fire until the moisture has completely evaporated.
Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg, and add the chopped parsley, mixing thoroughly.
Transfer to a bowl, cover with a piece of white, buttered paper, and put aside until wanted.

entries.title: DUXELLE FOR STUFFED VEGETABLES (Tomatoes, Mushrooms, &c.)
ingredients: 3 tbsp (45 ml) of half-glaze sauce with tomato
Crushed garlic the size of a pea
1 tbsp (15 ml) of fine, fresh bread-crumbs (optional)
instructions: Put the dry duxelle into a small stewpan. (Recipe 223)
Add the half-glaze sauce with tomato, crushed garlic, and white wine.
Set to simmer until the required degree of consistence is reached.
A tablespoonful of fine, fresh bread-crumbs may be added to thicken it.

entries.title: DUXELLE FOR GARNISHING SMALL PIES, ONIONS, CUCUMBERS, ETC.
ingredients: 4 tbsp (60 ml) of ordinary pork forcemeat (Recipe 196)
instructions: Combine the dry duxelle with the ordinary pork forcemeat. (Recipe 223, 196)

entries.title: MAINTENON (preparation used in stuffing preparations à la Maintenon)
ingredients: 4 tbsp (60 ml) of minced mushrooms, cooked
instructions: Put the Béchamel and Soubise into a vegetable-pan and reduce to half while stirring over a brisk fire. (Recipe 104)
Thicken, away from the fire, by means of the egg yolks.
Add the minced mushrooms, either cooked in the ordinary way or stewed in butter.

entries.title: MATIGNON
entries.introduction: This preparation serves chiefly for covering certain large joints of butcher’s meat, or fowl, to which it imparts an appropriate flavour.
ingredients: 2 medium carrots (red part only), finely minced
2 sticks of celery (from the heart), finely minced
1 tbsp (15 ml) of raw lean ham, cut paysanne-fashion
instructions: Combine the minced carrots, onions, celery, ham, thyme, and bay leaf.
Stew in butter.
Finally, swill the saucepan with the Madeira.

entries.title: MIREPOIX
introduction: The purpose of Mirepoix in culinary preparations is the same as that of Matignon, but its mode of use is different. (Recipe 227)
Its constituents are the same as those of the Matignon, but instead of being minced they are cut up into more or less fine dice, in accordance with the use for which the preparation is intended.
Instead of the ham, fresh and slightly-salted breast of pork may be used, while both the ham and the bacon may be excluded under certain circumstances.

entries.title: FINE OR BORDELAISE MIREPOIX
entries.introduction: This chiefly serves as an adjunct to crayfish and lobsters and is made in advance.
ingredients: 4 oz (113 g) of the red part only of carrots, diced or chopped
4 oz (113 g) of onion, diced or chopped
1 oz (28 g) of parsley stalks, diced or chopped
instructions: Cut the carrots, onion, and parsley stalks into dice. For a finer Mirepoix, they may be chopped and then thoroughly pressed in a towel to squeeze out their moisture.
Put the ingredients into a small stewpan with the butter and a little powdered thyme and bay.
Stew until all are well cooked.
Turn the preparation out into a small bowl, heap it together, cover it with a piece of white, buttered paper, and put aside until wanted.

entries.title: VARIOUS SALPICONS
introduction: This term stands for a certain preparatory method applied to a series of preparations.

Salpicons are simple or compound. Simple if they only contain one product, such as the meat of a fowl, game, butcher’s meat, foie gras, fish, ham, tongue, mushrooms, truffles, &c. Compound if they consist of two or more suitable ingredients.
The preparatory method consists in cutting the various ingredients into dice.
The series of preparations arises from the many possible combinations of the products, each bearing its own name (e.g., Royal, Financier, Chasseur, Parisien, Montglas, &c.).
Salpicons are always incorporated with a vehicular sauce which is in accordance with their constituents.

entries.title: BATTER FOR VARIOUS FRITTERS
ingredients: 1 tbsp (15 ml) of oil or melted butter
2 egg whites, whisked to a froth
instructions: Put the flour, salt, and oil/butter into a bowl. Add the necessary quantity of lukewarm water.
If the batter is to be used at once, mix the ingredients by turning them over and over without stirring with a spoon to avoid elasticity.
If the batter is prepared beforehand, it may be stirred, as it loses its elasticity when left to stand.
Before using, add the whisked egg whites.

entries.title: BATTER FOR VEGETABLES (Salsify, Celery, &c.)
ingredients: 2 tbsp (30 ml) of oil or melted butter
instructions: Put the flour, salt, and oil/butter into a bowl.
Dilute with the egg and the necessary quantity of cold water.
Keep this batter somewhat thin, do not stir it, and let it rest for a few hours before using.

entries.title: BATTER FOR FRUIT AND FLOWER FRITTERS
ingredients: 2 tbsp (30 ml) of oil or melted butter
2 egg whites, whisked to a froth
instructions: Put the flour, salt, and oil/butter into a bowl.
Dilute gradually with the beer and a little tepid water.
When about to use the batter, mix therewith the whisked egg whites.
notes: Keep this batter thin, if anything, and above all do not stir overmuch.

entries.title: BATTER FOR OVEN-GLAZED FRUIT FRITTERS
instructions: Mix the flour with the oil, salt, eggs (added one after the other), the necessary quantity of water, and the sugar.
Keep this preparation in a lukewarm place to let it ferment.
Stir it with a wooden spoon before using it to immerse the solids.
notes: Batter for fruit fritters may contain a few tablespoonfuls of brandy, in which case an equal quantity of the water must be suppressed.

entries.title: PROVENÇALE (preparation for stuffing cutlets à la Provençale)
ingredients: A crushed piece of garlic as large as a pea
instructions: Put the Béchamel into a vegetable-pan and reduce it until it has become quite dense.
Thicken it with the egg yolks.
Finish it away from the fire with the crushed garlic and grated cheese.
